title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge of current Greater Boston events.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's General Assignment Editor since 2019.
The quiz covers trending stories to help residents stay informed about local news.

title: Citywide needle pickup requests fluctuated from 2020 to 2025, but surged in these neighborhoods
author: Sana Muneer
bullets: Needle pickup requests in Boston surged 71% from 2022 to 2025, yet were still 17% below 2020 levels.
Jamaica Plain saw a 149% increase in requests, while South End had the highest volume, declining from 36% to 29% of citywide requests.
The city's mobile sharps team operates daily, conducting proactive sweeps and responding to 311 calls across Boston neighborhoods.

title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association in honor of her mother.
Sorting through her mother's kitchen items evokes memories for Cimo amidst her mother's Alzheimer's battle.
Cimo recalls her mother's nurturing through cooking, symbolized by objects like a yellow plastic pitcher.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins lost 6-3 to Nashville Predators, dropping their road record to 11-14-4 this season.
Bruins are currently the second Wild Card team in the Eastern Conference, just one point ahead of Columbus.
General Manager Don Sweeney is evaluating trade options, considering roster upgrades but wary of giving up future assets.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge David Barron issued a stay of removal for Seamus Culleton pending review of his deportation case.
Culleton has been detained in Texas since September for overstaying a 90-day visa from 2009.
Culleton, married to a U.S. citizen, has no criminal record in the U.S. and a pending green card application.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog died in an early morning house fire on Granite Street, Foxborough.
Foxborough Fire Department responded to the fire after a police officer reported smoke; firefighters from Mansfield and Plainville assisted.
The fire's cause is under investigation; the house sustained significant damage and was fully engulfed when responders arrived.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree canopy expanded by 151 acres from 2019 to 2024, according to a new citywide analysis.
East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury saw the largest tree canopy gains; Hyde Park and Mattapan had losses.
The canopy growth supports Boston's climate goals, aiming to reduce greenhouse emissions and enhance quality of life.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Robert Kraft's group committed to cover $7.8 million security costs for 2026 World Cup games at Gillette Stadium.
Foxborough officials remain unconvinced, questioning BS26's financial capability and security funding assurances for World Cup events.
Foxborough Select Board set a March 17 deadline to secure the funds and decide on granting an event license.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway is closing permanently this weekend after nearly a decade in business.
Rising insurance costs prompted the closure, with liquor liability insurance quotes rising significantly for the bar.
The tavern, popular with Northeastern University students, changed names and ownership in 2017, under Chuck Hitchcock and others.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum could return to the Celtics lineup against the Dallas Mavericks after nearly 10 months recovering from a ruptured Achilles tendon.
The Celtics listed Tatum as 'questionable' for the first time this season ahead of Friday's home game against the Mavericks.
Coach Joe Mazzulla may implement a minutes restriction and adjust rotations as Tatum returns to an already strong Celtics squad.
